records.ety: [L. exaltare; ex out (intens.) + altare to make high, altus high: cf.F. exalter. See Altitude.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Exalted; p. pr. & vb. n. Exalting.]

defs: To raise high; to elevate; to lift up.
quotes: I will exalt my throne above the stars of God.
Exalt thy towery head, and lift thine eyes

defs: To elevate in rank, dignity, power, wealth, character, or the like; to dignify; to promote; as, to exalt a prince to the throne, a citizen to the presidency.
quotes: He that humbleth himself shall be exalted.

defs: To elevate by praise or estimation; to magnify; to extol; to glorify.
quotes: In his own grace he doth exalt himself.

defs: To lift up with joy, pride, or success; to inspire with delight or satisfaction; to elate.
quotes: They who thought they got whatsoever he lost were mightily exalted.

defs: To elevate the tone of, as of the voice or a musical instrument.
quotes: Now Mars, she said, let Fame exalt her voice.

defs: To render pure or refined; to intensify or concentrate; as, to exalt the juices of bodies.
quotes: With chemic art exalts the mineral powers.
